title: Urceolina formosa
taxonomy: Urceolina formosa is classified in the family Amaryllidaceae. This family is part of the larger order Asparagales. Asparagales is one of the 64 orders in the class Liliopsida. The class Liliopsida is a subgroup of the division Magnoliophyta. The division Magnoliophyta is a part of the kingdom Plantae, which includes all flowering plants.

intro: Urceolina formosa, also known as Urceolina or Mexican Urceolina, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as pain relief, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Urceolina formosa include alkaloids like urceoline and urceoline N-oxide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Urceolina is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
